code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, compact (ideally single) sentence.
    """
    import re

    # Mapping from predicate to a short phrase (subject already omitted in later clauses)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "started construction in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
    }

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Helper to turn a single triple into a phrase (subject already known)
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    def phrase_from_triple(t: Triple) -> str:
        verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, "has")
        obj = t.object.strip('"')
        if t.predicate == "height":
            obj = re.sub(r"\s*\(.*?\)", "", obj).strip()
            obj = obj.replace("metres", "metres")
            return f"{verb} {obj}"
        if t.predicate in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
            return f"{verb} {obj} floors"
        return f"{verb} {obj}"

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Group triples by subject for easy lookup
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    subjects: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subjects.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Build description for each subject, but keep them in a flat list of clauses
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    clauses: list[str] = []

    for subj, sub_triples in subjects.items():
        # Separate location (if any) because we want to embed possible sub‑subject info
        loc_triple = next((t for t in sub_triples if t.predicate == "location"), None)

        # Start clause with the subject
        clause = subj

        # Add location phrase
        if loc_triple:
            loc_obj = loc_triple.object.strip('"')
            # If the location itself is a known subject, embed its attributes in a relative clause
            if loc_obj in subjects:
                # Gather non‑location attributes of the location subject
                rel_parts = []
                for lt in subjects[loc_obj]:
                    if lt.predicate == "location":
                        continue
                    rel_parts.append(phrase_from_triple(lt))
                if rel_parts:
                    clause += f" in {loc_obj} which " + ", ".join(rel_parts)
                else:
                    clause += f" in {loc_obj}"
            else:
                clause += f" in {loc_obj}"

        # Add remaining predicates
        for t in sub_triples:
            if t.predicate == "location":
                continue
            clause += f", {phrase_from_triple(t)}"

        # Capitalize first character and finish with a period
        clause = clause[0].upper() + clause[1:] + "."
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join all clauses into a single paragraph
    return " ".join(clauses)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
